userMessage: add more plots graphs etc if possible relvent to this

response.text:
Done — the scope now shows the original eye-analysis plots plus added scalar measurements on Ref1. The main eye diagram remains open, centered near 0 V, with eye width markers around 3341/4995 and 3341/4995 crossings and midlevel about 80.36 mV. The left side still shows the TIE spectrum, TIE histogram, and bathtub plot for Meas 1. The waveform view below shows a dense offline Ref1 waveform spanning roughly ±550 mV.

On the right measurement tiles, Meas 3 is Frequency with mean about 1.106 GHz, sigma about 298.4 MHz, max about 1.266 GHz, min about 359.0 MHz, N=1874. Newly added measurements are Meas 4 Rise Time at about 145.4 ps, Meas 5 Fall Time at about 220.4 ps, Meas 6 Amplitude at about 744.8 mV, Meas 7 Peak-to-Peak at about 1.104 V, and Meas 8 also shows Peak-to-Peak at about 1.104 V. The eye is visibly asymmetric, consistent with the slower fall time than rise time, and the jitter-related plots still indicate a mix of deterministic and periodic jitter components.
